title: Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae and the genus Chrysanthemum, but it is actually more accurately classified as a member of the genus Dendranthema or as a member of the genus Chrysanthemum within the subgenus Dendranthema, within the family Asteraceae. The classification of Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum can be further broken down into its botanical classification, which is a species within the subgenus Dendranthema. Some sources may classify Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum as a synonym of Dendranthema indicum. The species Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum is a type of flowering plant native to certain parts of Asia. Its classification is subject to ongoing research and may be subject to revision.

intro: Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum, also known as the three-pinnate chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is traditionally used in Chinese medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to calm the mind and reduce stress, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum tripinnatisectum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions related to stress and inflammation.
